column10: I use AI for different writing, research and interviewing tasks. I provide ChatGPT or Jasper a specific health topic, and ask it to come up with effective and relevant questions for a SME (subject matter expert). I also have AI write first drafts after I provide extensive information in the prompts, including preliminary data on a health topic and answers to questions from an expert. Jasper and ChatGPT will come up with first drafts, which I then edit thoroughly. Or I may have the AI tool make additions or edits before I make the final edits. I also use AI tools, including Gemini, for deep research on different health topics. Overall, the AI tools save me a considerable amount of time. All content is approved by SMEs, so there are layers of editing/reviewing that prevent any inaccurate information from being published.

Here’s a **consolidated playbook** of how I have been leveraging AI at work already, grouped into clear categories for quick reference:

# 📘 Patty’s Playbook

## 🔍 Research & Knowledge Checks

* Verify **branding and naming conventions** (e.g., FIU Herbert Wertheim College of Medicine).
* Fact-check **organizational processes** and ensure accurate terminology.
* Gather **contextual insights** about projects, procedures and internal updates.

---

## 🗂️ Work Support & Documentation

* **Locate and summarize documents** (trackers, updates, project notes).
* Provide **meeting or conversation summaries** to extract action items.
* Clarify **internal workflows** and step-by-step processes.

---

## ✍️ Writing & Editing

* Draft professional communications (emails, summaries, formal documents).
* Edit for **tone, clarity, and professionalism**.
* Maintain **consistency with institutional style** and formatting.

---

## 📊 Organization & Tracking

* Outline **task lists and processes** for clarity and execution.
* Provide **reminders** for follow-ups and deadlines.
* Build structured **checklists or reference guides** to stay organized.

column10: I use AI for nearly everything—copy, presentations, charts, and reports. I had a free account before, but once we got the Baptist Health account, the difference was clear. In my experience, the paid version is more detailed and accurate, which even prompted me to subscribe for my personal use.
